question: What vacation role does each person play on a group trip?

values: The Navigator – has the map in their head
The Foodie – googles restaurants instead of sights
The Photographer – needs good light, needs patience
The Party Animal – night > day
The Culture Nerd – knows every museum
The Beach Veggie – no plan, just lying down
The Complication Magnet – loses passport, phone, friends
Minimal packer – hand luggage for 2 weeks
Suitcase hoarder – 3 outfits a day
Last-minute disaster – packs in the taxi
Pharmacy on wheels – has something for everyone
Forgets the essentials – passport, charger, toothbrush
Perfectly organized – packing cubes, lists, backups
Packs more books than clothes

question: Which country truly suits each person?

values: Japan – order and respect
Italy – loud hands, good food
Iceland – alone with yourself and a sheep
Thailand – chaos and beach
Portugal – chill and nice tiles
Mexico – energy without a break
Scotland – rain and whisky
South Africa – nature to the horizon

question: What travel mode is each person?

values: Backpacker – flip-flops and a book
All-inclusive – wristband and lounger
Road trip – car full, no plans
City hopper – new city every 2 days
Hiker – up every mountain
Digital nomad – laptop by the pool
Luxury – spa, butler, quiet please

question: What's your perfect group travel destination?
options: City trip with lots of culture
Road trip with no fixed plan
A festival or event as the main reason

question: What's guaranteed to happen on our trip?
What has to be part of every group trip?
options: A chill day with no program
A moment people talk about for years

question: Who actually organizes the trip – even if others claim they do?
Who causes the first drama on vacation?
Who haggles hardest at the market?
Who would be the first to lose their passport?
Who could you really survive 3 weeks in close quarters with?
How well would we survive a 3-week trip as a group without fighting?
